channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: TMC - Defects - Signs & Street Furniture problem, 2nd June
item.description:
2 hr Damaged / missing bollard. Please make safe. X 3 1m barriers, x 3 sandbags Please collect damaged bollard, see attached picture Standard TM with temp FW closure. (Damaged Bollard)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked drain, 2nd June
item.description:
The Gully outside No 6 Coburn Gardens close to No 8 it’s not the gully pot it’s the outlet drain which is the problem, your gully crew only clear the pots of debis there schedule does not include jetting or testing each drain. Due to a number of cars parked in the highway by 4/6/8 Coburn belonging to employees of GCHQ and not residents your crews could only service gully’s they could get close to. This problem is a long term issue there is also root infiltration in the main outlet pipe before it discharges into the attenuation area that needs attention.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Coburn Gardens, Hatherley, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface problem, 2nd June
item.description:
Kerb edge
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Alleyway (Binyon Rd / Back Lane, Winchcombe), 2nd June
item.description:
Alley between Binyon Road and Back Lane in Winchcombe. This area has been raised by residents due to its current poor condition, which is creating difficulties for pedestrians, including parents with pushchairs and children travelling to and from school/nursery. There was some confusion locally regarding responsibility, which we have now established falls under Gloucestershire County Council Highways. To assist in identifying the exact location, please see the what3words reference below: ///dress.harp.lifeguard https://w3w.co/dress.harp.lifeguard is responsible for its upkeep and maintenance. I would appreciate your assistance in taking appropriate action to address the overgrown condition of the alley. Thank you for your help, and I look forward to your response

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Abbey View, Winchcombe, Winchcombe, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked drain causing flooding on road, 2nd June
item.description:
Drain completely blocked by silt and debris from the potholes. Now causing flooding issues, splashing pedestrians and impacting traffic.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 105, Village Road, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The grass verges are now extremely high and dangerous restricting visibility at both ends of the chicane. It is impossible to see oncoming traffic before going through the chicane. This could lead to a serious accident. Please cut the grass and ensure the cutting is done on a regular schedule throughout the spring and summer, 2nd June
item.description:
Next to and between the 2 chicanes on Gotherington Lane (adjacent to the new community building)
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gotherington Lane, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
A caravan has been abandoned on the road outside my house.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ashbrittle Road, Hucclecote, Tewkesbury

Welch road Gl510eq Cheltenham a traffic sign has fallen down the pole

item.category: Sign bracket is hanging/fallen off (Non illuminating sign)

item.title: Historic kerbstones destroyed/removed in Cudnall St conservation area., 1st June
item.description:
In 2 locations in Cudnall St, there are damaged and now missing kerb stones following works by National Grid. Following restoration of the pavement, some kerbstones fell into the road, but are no longer there. The kerbstones are referred to in the Local Plan for their importance in the Cudnall St conservation area

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34, Cudnall Street, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unauthorised painting on pavement, 1st June
item.description:
Unauthorised "keep clear" painted on pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 126, Broad Oak Way, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Community Improvement Group - Hartlebury Way and Pembridge Close, 1st June
item.description:
I have sent several emails to CBC and completed your (highways) online form but have not received a response. Please confirm who I should be contacting. Together with other residents, we are proposing to set up a community group to improve the appearance of our streets. Our first project will be to make a wildflower bed on the corner of Pembridge Close and Hartlebury Way, which is currently grass. Other projects we are thinking about include - planting spring bulbs on the grass verges - improving the overgrown area of land by the railings opposite 1 to 9 Hartlebury Way - litter picking - cleaning up the river behind the houses at 20 - 42 Hartlebury Way - improving the overgrown area of land alongside the alleyway adjacent to Glenfall School field
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Hartlebury Way, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
